meaning: n.[闊砞鍥哄畾闊冲瀷(鎸囦笉鏂弽澶嶇殑闊冲瀷)
examples: A jazz ostinato; usually provides a background for a solo improvisation.
New Orleans master mentor Ellis Marsalis played an exploratory, growing meditation on "Winter Wonderland, " anchored by brooding left-hand ostinato chords.
The next track, "Crystalised, " employs a familiar strategy: hooky ostinato riff embellished with a few subtle effects, leading to a soft vocal.
